sn.s: Ich han en Notfall und bruuch Hilf.
sn.e: I have an emergency and need help.

sn.s: Min Computer isch kapütt, grad vor ere wichtige Arbet.
sn.e: My computer broke, right before an important job.

sn.s: Ich lüte de Lukas aa, es bitzli nervös.
sn.e: I call Lukas, a little nervous.

sn.s: "Lukas, chan ich di um en grosse Gfalle bitte?"
sn.e: "Lukas, can I ask you a big favor?"
"Sure, tell me. What do you need?"

sn.s: "Chönntsch mer din Computer für e paar Täg uusliehe?"
sn.e: "Could you lend me your computer for a few days?"

sn.s: "Ich weiss, das isch viel verlangt," füeg ich dezue.
sn.e: "I know that's a lot to ask," I add.
"No trouble. That's what friends are for."

sn.s: "Ich gib der en guet acht und bring en am Fritig zrugg."
sn.e: "I'll take good care of it and bring it back Friday."

sn.s: "Ruhig, ich vertraue der," seit de Lukas.
En grosse Gfalle frage bruucht Vertraue.
sn.e: Asking a big favor requires trust.
And keeping your promise maintains it.
'Could you' and 'thanks' open a lot of doors.
